title: plan-executor compiled-plan manifest
description: Structured output of the plan-executor:compile-plan skill. Read and dispatched deterministically by the Rust scheduler. One LLM call produces this; no LLM invocation should mutate it after compile time.

version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

title.description: Short conventional-commit-subject summary of the plan, used as the PR title body. Sized to leave room for the `feat(JIRA-1234): ` prefix in a 72-char title; the orchestrator's PR_TITLE_GOAL_MAX_LEN constant tracks this maxLength. Optional for back-compat with manifests compiled before this field existed; the orchestrator falls back to truncating `goal` when absent.

type.description: Plan type drives default review/validation sets.

jira.description: JIRA ticket ID (e.g. CCP-123). Empty string if none.

target_repo.description: GitHub owner/repo slug where changes will be committed. Null for local-only runs.

target_branch.description: Feature branch to create/checkout for this plan.

path.description: Absolute path to the source plan markdown file. Embedded so the orchestrator can reference the plan without external lookups.

status.description: Execution state of the plan. Lives in the manifest, not in the plan markdown.

execution_mode.description: Where to run the plan. 'local' executes the Rust scheduler in the current process; 'remote' submits a job-spec.json to the configured remote_repo and lets a GitHub Actions runner execute it. Lives in the manifest, not in the plan markdown.

language.description: Project language. Drives the default `integration_testing` test command: rust→`cargo test --workspace`, node→`npm test --silent`, python→`python -m pytest -q`, go→`go test ./...`. Plans that don't need orchestrator-level integration tests should omit `integration_testing` from `pipeline.steps` instead. Optional for back-compat: when absent, the step falls back to detecting the language from marker files (Cargo.toml, package.json, etc.) in the working directory.

integration_test_command.description: Explicit override for the orchestrator-level integration-test command. When set, takes priority over `language`. Use for monorepos where the test entry point is in a sub-package, custom test runners (pnpm, bazel, etc.), or any project that can't be expressed by the language defaults.

program.description: Executable to spawn. Resolved via PATH.

args.description: Arguments passed after the program. May be empty.

workdir.description: Optional working directory. When omitted, the step uses ctx.workdir. Relative paths are resolved against ctx.workdir.

skip_code_review.description: Legacy flag retained for backwards compatibility with handover meta.json. New manifests should omit `code_review` from `pipeline.steps` instead — that's the authoritative way to disable a step today.

pipeline.description: Optional override for the daemon's plan pipeline. When omitted or null, the registry uses the default 7-step sequence (preflight → wave_execution → integration_testing → validation → pr_creation → pr_finalize → summary). Listing `code_review` between integration_testing and validation re-enables review once the dispatch→triage protocol is wired in. Order is preserved verbatim; unknown step names fail manifest validation in the Rust scheduler.

waves.description: Ordered list of execution waves. Each wave runs its task_ids in parallel. A wave starts when every wave in its depends_on array has completed successfully.

id.description: Unique wave ID. Fix-loop waves use IDs >= 100.

tasks.description: Map from task_id -> task spec. Every task_id referenced in waves[].task_ids MUST exist here.

prompt_file.description: Relative path (from tasks.json's directory) to the sub-task prompt markdown.

agent_type.description: Dispatcher picks the CLI based on this value. Enum is locked to what handoff.rs::AgentType supports.

can_fail.description: If true, a non-zero exit from this sub-agent is logged but does not fail the wave. Used for reviewer-diversity tasks (codex/gemini) where the tool may be unavailable.

description.description: One-line human summary. Not read by the executor; for the compile-then-inspect step.
